title: Implement storefront customer session state
verify: Client starts allowlisted provider login, retrieves current customer, and logs out using credentials include.

Provider login accepts only exact paths https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth and https://id.vk.com/authorize with no explicit port, credentials, or fragment; backend destinations and all other paths fail closed.
Auth state follows the normative lifecycle and treats current-customer 401/session expiry as guest.
Safe return-path adapter accepts only one-leading-slash internal paths and consumes the versioned sessionStorage value.
Confirmed logout clears customer/auth state and local cart reference without storing JWT/provider tokens.

verify.reason: Independent T3 verification found stale current-customer/logout races, weak provider-origin validation, repeatable return-path consumption, and cart-reference cleanup failure handling.
T3 re-verification found return-path replay after getItem failure and lost confirmed customer on failed logout overlapping restore.
Final T3 verification found storefront backend-origin-only validation rejects the VK provider authorization redirect; retry budget 2/2 is exhausted.
Operator approved exact Google/VK authorization origins and one reviewed recovery attempt beyond the default retry budget.
Operator-approved recovery failed T3 verification: backend callback paths were overbroad, explicit default ports were accepted, and fragment payloads bypassed redirect-data rejection.
Operator approved exact provider authorization paths, complete backend-destination rejection, and one additional reviewed recovery attempt.
Recovery 2 failed T3 verification because malformed and double-encoded OAuth query parameters were accepted fail-open.
TASK-043 closed the remaining malformed-query gap with T3 functional and semantic PASS.
Final full verification found logout 401 state cleanup and empty-fragment rejection defects.
Full T3 functional and semantic verification passed after TASK-043 remediation and final bounded recovery fixes.

evidence_required: Auth client/state and safe return-path test output.
Storefront typecheck and Memory Bank lint output.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Use direct Medusa HTTP session APIs and credentials include; do not add JS SDK unless implementation proves it smaller.
Persist only safe return path in sessionStorage; auth tokens are forbidden.
Use existing CartProvider clearLocalReference on confirmed logout.

OAuth start destinations are limited to exact paths https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth and https://id.vk.com/authorize; backend destinations, explicit ports, credentials, fragments, and all other paths are forbidden.
invariants: Client auth state never proves backend identity without current-customer success.
Logout is not reported successful before session destruction succeeds.
Unsafe return paths fall back to root.
purpose: Provide one deterministic session-backed customer state boundary for login UI and checkout gating.
success_outcome: Storefront can start login, resolve session customer, store only safe navigation state, and log out without token or cart leakage.
anti_goals: Do not implement provider callback backend logic.
Do not implement login page or checkout page markup.
Do not persist JWTs or provider tokens.

stop_conditions: Session API requires browser token persistence.
Safe return path cannot be isolated from provider callback data.
Logout cannot clear the existing cart reference through its public provider boundary.
